title: stdlib sort recursion depth is O(n): cannot sort >= ~100k elements
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). lib/core/list/sort.esk recursion depth grows linearly with list length: n=50000 sorts fine; n=99999 SIGILLs (rc 132, no message - raw stack blowout under the depth guard); n>=100001 raises 'maximum recursion depth (100000) exceeded'. A merge sort with O(log n) control depth (or iterative bottom-up merge) fixes both failure modes. ALSO: sorting 50k elements peaks at ~983MB RSS (measured by data_sort_50k.esk budgets) - the rewrite should fix the allocation profile too.
acceptance: (sort 100k-list <) returns a sorted list under -r and AOT (tests/stress/found/sort_100k.esk)
campaign_payoff: 100k-element vector/list sort is an explicit v1.3 P4 scale scenario.
